task: M4: implement the 2 non-HMAC drivers (providers/gcs + providers/azure) — the abstraction stress test for OAuth2 / SharedKey / SAS / User Delegation. Per provider_roadmap.md M4 exit checklist: NO new Code/Capability/DirectGrantMode may be added to pkg/uos (frozen surface enforced by TestFrozenSurface). Any abstraction-pressure point discovered must be logged to provider_roadmap.md 'Lessons (M4)' instead of growing pkg/uos. Update matrix + CHANGELOG + RELEASING. Tag commands maintainer-gated.
scope_note: M4 = 2 providers in parallel via Ralph (same shape as M3 phase 2). M5 (qiniu+upyun, the DirectGrant-non-URL milestone) is the next session. v0.2.0 retrofit (architect's 4 optimization notes + httpx flake fix) deferred until M4 informs whether the s3common abstraction needs widening for non-S3 vendors.

stories.title: providers/gcs native driver (cloud.google.com/go/storage)
acceptanceCriteria: providers/gcs/ scaffolded via scripts/add-provider.sh gcs (go.mod with module github.com/maqian/object-storage-client/providers/gcs + parent + testkit requires + replace directives).
providers/gcs/go.mod requires cloud.google.com/go/storage directly; no other cloud SDKs. Pin Go version that the SDK requires (likely 1.22+).

factory.go implements uos.Factory: Provider() = 'gcs'; Validate accepts CredentialProvider (OAuth2/Service Account/ADC chain) OR HMAC keys (when AuthScheme=AuthHMAC); Open builds the storage.Client with the resolved credential, optional custom endpoint (for emulator / regional override), and disables the SDK's internal retryer (storage.WithBackoff(gax.Backoff{Max: 0}) or equivalent).

driver.go implements full uos.Client + 4 sub-services using cloud.google.com/go/storage. Multipart: GCS does NOT have S3 multipart; instead it has resumable uploads. Map MultipartService to the resumable-upload session API (one InitiateMultipartUpload returns a session URL; UploadPart writes a chunk to the session; CompleteMultipartUpload finalizes). Bypasses transfer.Manager. If the resumable-upload semantics diverge irreconcilably from the S3 multipart contract, document the gap in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M4) BEFORE implementing — do not silently warp the unified surface.

Metadata key prefix: GCS uses arbitrary metadata keys (no x-goog-meta-* enforcement at the SDK level); use s3common.LowerMetadataKeys for case-folding to keep cross-provider behavior consistent. Document in driver doc.

Signer.SignURL via storage.SignedURL with V4 signing. If the resolved credential lacks a private key (e.g., ADC without a key file, ComputeEngine credentials), SignURL returns *uos.Error{Code: ErrUnsupported, Capability: CapSignedURLRead, Reason: 'credential lacks signing key'}. SignedURLWrite has the same gating. Signer.IssueDirectGrant returns ErrUnsupported{CapDirectGrant} per matrix footnote 5 (GCS issues writes as URL, no token grant).

error_map.go: GCS errors come back as *googleapi.Error with HTTP status + Code string. Use s3common.MapHTTPStatus + s3common.MapContextErr; GCS Code strings are vendor-specific, so DO NOT add them to s3common.MapCodeString (that's S3-family only) — instead handle them in a local switch in error_map.go. If any frequently-needed pattern emerges, document it in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M4) for v0.2.0 consideration.

capabilities.go returns the 13-cell Report matching the gcs column in docs/provider_matrix.md: 7 ✅ (BucketCRUD, ObjectCRUD, ListPrefixDelimiter, RangeRead, MultipartUpload, ObjectTagging, ManagedEncryption, Versioning) + 2 🟡¹ (SignedURLRead, SignedURLWrite — credential-dependent) + 1 ❌⁵ (DirectGrant) + 1 🟡¹⁰ (ObjectACL — Uniform vs Fine-grained) + 1 🧩¹² (NativeMove). NOTE: Versioning ⏳M4 → ✅ (GCS object versioning is bucket-level boolean like S3, but the API model is generation-number-based and works without bucket setup); document the semantic in capabilities.go doc.

driver_test.go (//go:build docker): TestRunSuite SKIPs by default (GCS XML/JSON dialect ≠ MinIO S3 SigV4); cloud-nightly env vars OMC_GCS_NIGHTLY_KEY (path to service account JSON) + OMC_GCS_NIGHTLY_BUCKET + OMC_GCS_NIGHTLY_PROJECT gate the real-GCS contract suite. TestSpawnMinIOSmoke runs in PR gate (validates testkit wiring).
From providers/gcs: go build / go vet / gofmt -l . silent. From root: TestFrozenSurface 3 subtests pass (no Code/Cap/DirectGrantMode added — this is the M4 hard fence).

Report any abstraction-pressure point discovered (e.g., 'resumable upload doesn't fit Multipart contract', 'OAuth2 credential rotation isn't expressible in CredentialProvider') in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M4).

stories.title: providers/azure native driver (azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/storage/azblob)
acceptanceCriteria: providers/azure/ scaffolded; go.mod requires github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/storage/azblob directly. Pin Go version that the SDK requires.

factory.go implements uos.Factory: Provider() = 'azure'; Validate requires DriverConfig.StorageAccount (Azure has no S3-style 'region' — the storage account encodes the location); CredentialProvider may yield SharedKey (AccountName+AccountKey), SAS (token string), or AuthCustom (Entra ID / user-delegation key). Open builds the appropriate azblob client variant: NewClientWithSharedKeyCredential / NewClientWithNoCredential+SAS / NewClientWithAzureADCredential.

driver.go implements full uos.Client + 4 sub-services using azblob. Bucket maps to Azure Container (1:1). Multipart: Azure uses Block Blob with PutBlock / PutBlockList. Map MultipartService to PutBlock+PutBlockList (each block has a base64-encoded ID; the 'parts' list becomes the BlockList in the Commit step). Bypasses transfer.Manager. Document any divergence (e.g., 4 MiB minimum block size on staging blocks vs S3's 5 MiB) in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M4) — do NOT widen pkg/uos to absorb.

Metadata key prefix: Azure uses x-ms-meta-* on the wire but the SDK strips the prefix in its Metadata map[string]string; use s3common.LowerMetadataKeys for case-folding (Azure metadata keys are case-insensitive but the SDK preserves user case).

Signer.SignURL: issues SAS via the azblob SAS package. account-key SAS works with AuthSharedKey; user-delegation SAS works with AuthCustom carrying an Entra-issued user-delegation key. SAS includes a start-time semantic (signedstart=…) that SignURL ignores — start-time is set to (now - 5 min) for clock-skew tolerance, expiry per request.ExpiresIn. Document this in Signer doc.

Signer.IssueDirectGrant: Azure SAS can be expressed as URL (via SignURL) OR as token+headers (DirectGrant). Implement both — DirectGrant returns *uos.DirectGrant with Mode=DirectGrantModeToken (one of the existing 4 frozen DirectGrantModes; verify it's already in the frozen set BEFORE implementing — if not, this is a hard stop and goes to Lessons (M4)). DirectGrant.Token = SAS string; DirectGrant.Headers may include x-ms-version. capabilities.go marks CapDirectGrant ✅ for Azure (validation moment for the frozen DirectGrantMode set).

error_map.go: azblob errors come back as *azcore.ResponseError with ErrorCode string (e.g., 'BlobNotFound', 'ContainerNotFound', 'AuthenticationFailed'). Use s3common.MapHTTPStatus + s3common.MapContextErr. Azure ErrorCodes are vendor-specific — handle in a local switch in error_map.go (same pattern as gcs); DO NOT pollute s3common.MapCodeString.

capabilities.go returns the 13-cell Report matching the azure column: 6 ✅ (BucketCRUD, ObjectCRUD, ListPrefixDelimiter, RangeRead, MultipartUpload, ObjectTagging) + 1 ✅ DirectGrant⁶ (per matrix footnote 6 — Azure SAS as both URL and token-headers) + 2 🟡² (SignedURLRead, SignedURLWrite — SAS scheme-dependent) + 1 ❌¹¹ (ObjectACL — Azure has no S3-style per-object ACL; per footnote 11) + 1 🟡⁸ (Versioning — requires account-level enablement, per footnote 8) + 1 ⏳M4→✅ (ManagedEncryption — Azure server-side encryption is on by default; ✅ with note) + 1 🧩¹² (NativeMove). Total = 13.

driver_test.go (//go:build docker): TestRunSuite SKIPs by default (Azure SharedKey/SAS dialect ≠ MinIO S3 SigV4); cloud-nightly env vars OMC_AZURE_NIGHTLY_ACCOUNT + OMC_AZURE_NIGHTLY_KEY + OMC_AZURE_NIGHTLY_CONTAINER gate the real-Azure contract suite. TestSpawnMinIOSmoke runs in PR gate.
From providers/azure: go build / go vet / gofmt -l . silent. TestFrozenSurface still passes (the M4 hard fence).

Report any abstraction-pressure point in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M4): notably whether 'SAS includes start-time' fits in pkg/uos.Signer.SignURL request, and whether Block Blob's PutBlockList semantics fit cleanly into the Multipart abort/complete contract.

stories.title: Matrix + CHANGELOG + RELEASING + provider_roadmap (M4 lessons) update
acceptanceCriteria: docs/provider_matrix.md: gcs + azure driver-status rows updated to 'Shipped (v0.1.0)'.

docs/provider_matrix.md: 13 capability rows × 2 columns (gcs + azure) updated from ⏳M4 placeholders to actual symbols matching each driver's capabilities() return. The shape DIFFERS from S3-family — this is expected per M4 validation focus.

CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] gains entries for providers/gcs/v0.1.0 and providers/azure/v0.1.0 — each summarising SDK choice, auth-scheme model (OAuth2/SharedKey/SAS), the multipart-mapping decision (resumable upload for GCS / Block Blob for Azure), and any frozen-surface temptation deferred.
RELEASING.md §4: provider tag commands extended to include providers/gcs/v0.1.0 and providers/azure/v0.1.0.

docs/provider_roadmap.md 'Lessons (M4)' section filled in (replace the '_(to be filled)_' placeholder). MUST honestly state: (a) whether any abstraction-pressure point surfaced, and if so what it was; (b) whether v0.2.0 should consider widening any pkg/uos surface to absorb it; (c) the recommended deferral path (frozen-surface bump, helper extraction, or document-and-leave). If no abstraction defect surfaced, write 'no abstraction defect detected; gcs + azure shipped with zero pkg/uos change'.
AGENTS.md Appendix A status table — update item 4 (s3common scope) and add a new item if M4 lessons surfaced an abstraction question that should be tracked for v0.2.0.
go.work updated to list the 2 new modules (root + testkit + 6 S3-family + gcs + azure = 9 modules).
Final root-level go test -short -race ./... still green; TestFrozenSurface 3 subtests still pass.
